\chapter{analysis\_core}
\label{\detokenize{api/analysis_core:analysis-core}}\label{\detokenize{api/analysis_core::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Querschnitts\sphinxhyphen{} und Materialmethoden, Moment\sphinxhyphen{}Krümmungs\sphinxhyphen{}Analyse.


\section{section\_methods}
\label{\detokenize{api/analysis_core:module-core.analysis_core.section_methods}}\label{\detokenize{api/analysis_core:section-methods}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.analysis\_core.section\_methods@\spxentry{core.analysis\_core.section\_methods}}\index{core.analysis\_core.section\_methods@\spxentry{core.analysis\_core.section\_methods}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Moment\sphinxhyphen{}curvature and cross\sphinxhyphen{}section methods per Eurocode 2.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides routines for computing the cracking moment, the SLS/ULS bending
strength, and full as well as simplified moment\sphinxhyphen{}curvature (M\sphinxhyphen{}κ) diagrams,
plus helpers for building SLS/ULS sections and querying section properties.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Units: lengths in mm, forces in N, moments in Nmm.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Author: Elliot Melcer
\index{InvalidSectionForMKError@\spxentry{InvalidSectionForMKError}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/analysis_core:core.analysis_core.section_methods.InvalidSectionForMKError}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{exception}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.analysis\_core.section\_methods.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{InvalidSectionForMKError}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Raised when a section cannot produce a valid M\sphinxhyphen{}κ diagram.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Typical cause: prestress too high, so the section would crush before
cracking.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_cracking\_moment\_sls\_Nmm\_EC() (im Modul core.analysis\_core.section\_methods)@\spxentry{calculate\_cracking\_moment\_sls\_Nmm\_EC()}\spxextra{im Modul core.analysis\_core.section\_methods}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/analysis_core:core.analysis_core.section_methods.calculate_cracking_moment_sls_Nmm_EC}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.analysis\_core.section\_methods.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_cracking\_moment\_sls\_Nmm\_EC}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{section}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{0.0}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate the cracking moment of a section at SLS.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Finds the strain profile where the bottom fiber reaches the cracking
strain \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{eps\_ctm = fctm / Ecm}} while maintaining equilibrium with the
applied axial force \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{n}}. Includes physical strain\sphinxhyphen{}limit checks to
avoid spurious solutions where the concrete would crush before cracking.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameter}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{section}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GenericSection}}) – Section to analyze (should be a ULS section; an SLS section is
built internally).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{float}}}, \sphinxstyleemphasis{optional}) – Applied axial force {[}N{]} (positive = tension, negative =
compression). Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0.0}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Rückgabe}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar
Result with the keys:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{section}} : GenericSection — the SLS section used.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{m\_cr}} : float — cracking moment {[}Nmm{]}, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{float('\sphinxhyphen{}inf')}}
if the section crushes before cracking.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{strain\_profile}} : list — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}eps\_0, chi\_y, chi\_z{]}}} at cracking.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{valid}} : bool — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} if a physically valid solution was found.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{reason}} : str or None — explanation if invalid, else \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{chi\_min\_physical}} : float — present only on the invalid branch;
the lower physical curvature bound {[}1/mm{]}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{eps\_top}} : float — present only on the valid branch; top\sphinxhyphen{}fiber
strain {[}\sphinxhyphen{}{]} of the solution.

\end{itemize}


\sphinxlineitem{Rückgabetyp}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}

\sphinxlineitem{Verursacht}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If no concrete geometry is found in the section.

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strain profile convention: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{eps(z) = eps\_0 + chi\_y * z}}. For sagging
bending \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{chi\_y}} is negative, so the crushing limit acts as a lower
bound on the curvature.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_bending\_strength\_sls\_Nmm\_EC() (im Modul core.analysis\_core.section\_methods)@\spxentry{calculate\_bending\_strength\_sls\_Nmm\_EC()}\spxextra{im Modul core.analysis\_core.section\_methods}}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{section}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{GenericSection}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{0.0}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate the SLS bending strength of a section.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Builds an SLS section, computes the bending strength for the given
axial force, and returns the result together with the associated
strain profile.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameter}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{section}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GenericSection}}) – Section to analyze.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{float}}}, \sphinxstyleemphasis{optional}) – Applied axial force {[}N{]} (positive = tension). Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0.0}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Rückgabe}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar
Result with the keys:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{section}} : GenericSection — the SLS section used.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{m\_u}} : float or None — SLS bending strength {[}Nmm{]}, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}
if the moment cannot be taken by the section.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{strain\_profile}} : list or None — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}eps\_0, chi\_y, 0.0{]}}} at
ultimate, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} if invalid.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{valid}} : bool — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} if a valid solution was found.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{reason}} : str or None — failure reason if invalid, else \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}.

\end{itemize}


\sphinxlineitem{Rückgabetyp}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}

\sphinxlineitem{Verursacht}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – For any ValueError other than the section being unable to take the
    moment (such errors are treated as real bugs and re\sphinxhyphen{}raised).

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_bending\_strength\_uls\_Nmm\_EC() (im Modul core.analysis\_core.section\_methods)@\spxentry{calculate\_bending\_strength\_uls\_Nmm\_EC()}\spxextra{im Modul core.analysis\_core.section\_methods}}

{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate the ULS bending strength of a section.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Converts the input to a ULS section (so an SLS section may safely be
passed), computes the bending strength for the given axial force, and
returns the result together with the associated strain profile.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameter}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{section}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GenericSection}}) – Section to analyze (SLS or ULS; converted to ULS internally).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{float}}}, \sphinxstyleemphasis{optional}) – Applied axial force {[}N{]} (positive = tension). Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0.0}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Rückgabe}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar
Result with the keys:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{section}} : GenericSection — the ULS section used.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{m\_u}} : float or None — ULS bending strength {[}Nmm{]}, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}
if the moment cannot be taken by the section.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{strain\_profile}} : list or None — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}eps\_0, chi\_y, 0.0{]}}} at
ultimate, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} if invalid.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{valid}} : bool — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} if a valid solution was found.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{reason}} : str or None — failure reason if invalid, else \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}}.

\end{itemize}


\sphinxlineitem{Rückgabetyp}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}

\sphinxlineitem{Verursacht}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – For any ValueError other than the section being unable to take the
    moment (such errors are treated as real bugs and re\sphinxhyphen{}raised).

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

{{ $\rightarrow$ MomentCurvatureResults}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute the moment\sphinxhyphen{}curvature results for the given section.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dispatches to the full or the simplified M\sphinxhyphen{}κ method depending on
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{simplification}} and enforces a force\sphinxhyphen{}controlled (monotonically
increasing) result. For prestressed sections an initial state point
(κ₀, M=0) is added.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameter}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{section}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GenericSection}}) – Section to analyse (ULS).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{float}}}, \sphinxstyleemphasis{optional}) – Axial force {[}N{]}. Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{0.0}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{constitutive\_law}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str}}}, \sphinxstyleemphasis{optional}) – Keyword for the concrete constitutive law. Default:
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"TENSTIFF\_PARABOLIC"}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{simplification}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bool}}} or \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{int}}} or \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{float}}}, \sphinxstyleemphasis{optional}) – Controls the simplified M\sphinxhyphen{}κ calculation in
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_simplified\_moment\_curvature\_method()}}. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}} uses the full
method; \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} or a positive number uses the simplified method.
Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{debug}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}, \sphinxstyleemphasis{optional}) – Enables debug output. Default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Rückgabe}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The complete, force\sphinxhyphen{}controlled M\sphinxhyphen{}κ curve.

\sphinxlineitem{Rückgabetyp}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MomentCurvatureResults}}

\sphinxlineitem{Verursacht}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{simplification}} is neither \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{False}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{True}} nor a positive
    number.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_prestress\_forces\_Nmm() (im Modul core.analysis\_core.section\_methods)@\spxentry{calculate\_prestress\_forces\_Nmm()}\spxextra{im Modul core.analysis\_core.section\_methods}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/analysis_core:core.analysis_core.section_methods.calculate_prestress_forces_Nmm}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.analysis\_core.section\_methods.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_prestress\_forces\_Nmm}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{section}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{GenericSection}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#tuple}{tuple}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate the prestressing moment and total prestress force.

\sphinxAtStartPar
For each prestressed reinforcement the prestressing force is
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{F\_p = A\_s * eps\_ini * E\_s}} and the prestressing moment is
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{M\_p = Σ(F\_p * z\_s)}} about the section centroid.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameter}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{section}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GenericSection}}) – SLS section with prestressed reinforcement.

\sphinxlineitem{Rückgabe}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(M\_p, N\_p)}} where \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{M\_p}} is the prestressing moment {[}Nmm{]}
(always positive) and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{N\_p}} is the total prestress force {[}N{]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Rückgabetyp}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#tuple}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tuple}}} of \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{float}}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/analysis_core:core.analysis_core.section_methods.get_strain_at_point}}

\sphinxAtStartPar

\end{itemize}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.analysis\_core.material\_methods.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{CrackingConcreteLawEC}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{strains}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html\#numpy.ndarray}{ndarray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{stresses}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html\#numpy.ndarray}{ndarray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{eps\_cu1}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{eps\_c1}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{o}{**}\DUrole{n}{kwargs}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UserDefined}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Author: Elliot Melcer

\sphinxAtStartPar
A concrete constitutive law that handles tensile cracking correctly
in moment\sphinxhyphen{}curvature analysis according to Eurocode.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The class overrides get\_ultimate\_strain of UserDefined to allow for cracking:
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{get\_ultimate\_strain(yielding)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Called by get\_balanced\_failure\_strain() to determine chi\_ultimate.
Returns 1e6 as the positive (tensile) limit so that the concrete
self\sphinxhyphen{}pairing in the double loop never produces the governing
curvature.  chi\_ultimate is therefore always governed by the
reinforcement\sphinxhyphen{}concrete pair (reinf fracture vs. concrete crushing),
which is physically correct.

\end{description}
\index{get\_ultimate\_strain() (Methode von core.analysis\_core.material\_methods.CrackingConcreteLawEC)@\spxentry{get\_ultimate\_strain()}\spxextra{Methode von core.analysis\_core.material\_methods.CrackingConcreteLawEC}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/analysis_core:core.analysis_core.material_methods.CrackingConcreteLawEC.get_ultimate_strain}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_ultimate\_strain}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{yielding}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{bool}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return (compressive\_limit, 1e6).

\sphinxAtStartPar
The 1e6 tensile side is intentionally unreachable so that the
concrete self\sphinxhyphen{}pairing in get\_balanced\_failure\_strain() never
produces the minimum curvature.  chi\_ultimate ends up governed
by the reinforcement\sphinxhyphen{}concrete pair instead.

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Create a CFRP\sphinxhyphen{}Reinforcement object from materials registry.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Args:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
mat\_id: Material identifier (e.g., „solidian GRID Q142/142\sphinxhyphen{}CCE\sphinxhyphen{}25“)
materials: Materials registry dictionary
prestress\_percent: Prestress level as percentage (0\sphinxhyphen{}100)
gamma\_s: Partial safety factor for reinforcement

\sphinxlineitem{Returns:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Reinforcement object

\sphinxlineitem{Raises:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
KeyError: If mat\_id not found in registry
ValueError: If material type is not ‚reinforcement‘

\sphinxlineitem{Example:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
materials = load\_materials\_registry(„materials.csv“)
rebar = get\_reinforcement\_from\_registry(
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
„solidian GRID Q142/142\sphinxhyphen{}CCE\sphinxhyphen{}25“,
materials,
prestress\_percent=50.0
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
)

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Get raw material properties dictionary from registry.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Useful for accessing GWP, cost, and other properties not needed
for structural analysis but needed for objective function.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Args:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
mat\_id: Material identifier
materials: Materials registry dictionary

\sphinxlineitem{Returns:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dictionary with material properties

\sphinxlineitem{Raises:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
KeyError: If mat\_id not found in registry

\sphinxlineitem{Example:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
mat\_props = get\_material\_properties(„solidian GRID Q142/142\sphinxhyphen{}CCE\sphinxhyphen{}25“, materials)
gwp = mat\_props{[}„gwp“{]}  \# kg CO2\sphinxhyphen{}eq
cost = mat\_props{[}„cost“{]}  \# €/unit

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

Calculate deflections using Simpson’s rule and virtual work method.
Uses position\sphinxhyphen{}dependent M\sphinxhyphen{}κ curves (calculated at support and midspan).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Position convention: x is normalized (0 at first support, 1 at second support, etc.)

\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{constitutive\_law}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{'NONE'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{m\_k\_simplification}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{debug}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{bool}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{extended\_debug}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{bool}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate maximum deflection using Simpson’s rule and virtual work method.
Uses position\sphinxhyphen{}dependent M\sphinxhyphen{}κ curves.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameter}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{load\_history\_method}} – 
\sphinxAtStartPar
„NONE“      no load history considered, direct method used with parameter combination
„FACTOR\_EC“    load history considered according to Eurocode 2, Chapter 7.4.3, Equation (7.18)
\# „SECANT“    load history considered according to Kreller (1989)*, Chapter 4.2.4
\#
\# {\color{red}\bfseries{}*}Title: „Zum nichtlinearen Trag\sphinxhyphen{} und Verformungsverhalten von Stahlbetonstabtragwerken unter Last\sphinxhyphen{} und Zwangeinwirkung“


\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{m\_k\_simplification}} – Control simplified M\sphinxhyphen{}K\sphinxhyphen{}Diagram Calculation
For available inputs see \_simplified\_moment\_curvature\_method() in section\_methods.py

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{constitutive\_law}} – Control Concrete Material Behavior in SLS
for available inputs see sls\_concrete() in material\_methods.py

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{slab\_construction}} – Slab construction object

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{loads}} – Loads object

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{system}} – Structural system type

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{combination}} – Load combination (Ignored when load\_history\_method=‘FACTOR\_EC‘)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_intervals}} – Number of intervals for Simpson’s rule (must be even)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{N\_axial\_N}} – Axial force {[}N{]} (positive = tension)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{debug}} – Enable debug output

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{extended\_debug}} – Enable extended debug output

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Rückgabe}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum deflection {[}mm{]} Note: positive: sagging, negative: hogging

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}


\sphinxstepscope

\sphinxAtStartPar
Asserts that the slab’s infill material is the same object as the floor’s
infill layer material, if an infill layer is present in the floor.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Note: This is only relevant if slab construction is instantiated manually.
One must make sure to use the same infill object for both the slab and the floor layer
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Raises:}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{ValueError: If an infill layer exists in the floor but its material}
\sphinxAtStartPar
is not the same object as the slab’s infill material.

\end{description}

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This method returns the section of a one\sphinxhyphen{}way\sphinxhyphen{}slab at x.
Note: Input domain must be normalized to the following model:
\# x = 0 at first support, x = 1 at second support, etc.
\#
\#   {\color{red}\bfseries{}|\sphinxhyphen{}> x
\#   0      0.5      1      1.5      2
\#
\#   ====================================|}…
\#  /\_            /\_            /\_\#
:param x:
:return:

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\section{problem\_builder}
\label{\detokenize{api/ioh_core:module-core.ioh_core.problem_builder}}\label{\detokenize{api/ioh_core:problem-builder}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.ioh\_core.problem\_builder@\spxentry{core.ioh\_core.problem\_builder}}\index{core.ioh\_core.problem\_builder@\spxentry{core.ioh\_core.problem\_builder}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Author: Max Dombrowski
Modified by Elliot Melcer („Addition by: Elliot Melcer“ used to mark code)
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
pass materials to analysis

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Let the slab module drop constraints that don’t apply (i.e. hp\_slab: defl\_case\_sls a/b cuts out unwanted checks from logging)

\end{itemize}
\index{build\_problems\_for\_slab() (im Modul core.ioh\_core.problem\_builder)@\spxentry{build\_problems\_for\_slab()}\spxextra{im Modul core.ioh\_core.problem\_builder}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/ioh_core:core.ioh_core.problem_builder.build_problems_for_slab}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.ioh\_core.problem\_builder.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{build\_problems\_for\_slab}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{slab\_dir}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html\#pathlib.Path}{Path}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{slab\_module}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Build all IOH problems for a slab type directory and its analysis module.
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
CSV\sphinxhyphen{}driven: parameter\_defaults.csv, constraint\_defaults.csv, problem\_list.csv

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
One EvalContext per problem (per\sphinxhyphen{}problem\_ID), binding that problem’s constraints into analysis()

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
IOH constraints log \sphinxstyleemphasis{raw} violations from the cache (default: HIDDEN/1/1 unless overridden)

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{build\_problems\_for\_slab\_type() (im Modul core.ioh\_core.problem\_builder)@\spxentry{build\_problems\_for\_slab\_type()}\spxextra{im Modul core.ioh\_core.problem\_builder}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/ioh_core:core.ioh_core.problem_builder.build_problems_for_slab_type}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.ioh\_core.problem\_builder.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{build\_problems\_for\_slab\_type}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{slab\_type}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Build all problems for a given slab type string.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Valid types are discovered dynamically by scanning
‚slab\_benchmark.slabs‘ for subfolders with an ‚analysis.py‘.
If the provided slab\_type is invalid, a clear error lists all valid options.

\end{fulllineitems}



\section{cache\_eval}
\label{\detokenize{api/ioh_core:module-core.ioh_core.cache_eval}}\label{\detokenize{api/ioh_core:cache-eval}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.ioh\_core.cache\_eval@\spxentry{core.ioh\_core.cache\_eval}}\index{core.ioh\_core.cache\_eval@\spxentry{core.ioh\_core.cache\_eval}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.ioh\_core.import\_specs.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{load\_param\_defaults}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{path}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html\#pathlib.Path}{Path}}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Dict}{Dict}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Read parameter\_defaults.csv and return:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\{ name: \{domain, values, lb, ub, fixed\_idx, role, value\_type\} \}
or, for role=‘lookup‘:
\{ name: \{domain, values, role, value\_type, lookup\_key\} \}

\sphinxlineitem{Required columns:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
name;domain;values;grid\_start;grid\_step;grid\_count;
default\_lb;default\_ub;default\_fixed;role;value\_type;lookup\_key

\end{description}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{domain: ‚catalog‘ uses ‚values‘ as a {\color{red}\bfseries{}|}\sphinxhyphen{}separated list.}
\sphinxAtStartPar
‚grid‘ uses start/step/count to generate values.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{role: ‚var‘    — optimization variable (lb/ub active)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
‚fixed‘  — constant per problem (fixed\_idx active)
‚lookup‘ — derived from another parameter via lookup\_key;
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
never appears in problem\_list.csv, resolved in decode()
\end{quote}

\end{description}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
lb/ub/fixed\_idx are 0\sphinxhyphen{}based indices into ‚values‘.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
value\_type: ‚float‘, ‚int‘, or ‚str‘ (default: ‚float‘ if empty).

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{lookup\_key: only required for role=‘lookup‘; must name another parameter}
\sphinxAtStartPar
whose catalog/grid is positionally aligned with this one.

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Notes:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
‚type‘ indicates material category: reinforcement, infill, insulation, screed

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All numeric fields are converted to float (empty strings become None)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Commas in numbers (European format) are converted to periods

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
